text.title: Eight PMBoK 7 Performance Domains
text.summary: Eight interconnected domains that together define where project managers must achieve results, not just follow processes.

section.title: 1. Stakeholder
section.p: Purpose: effectively engage stakeholders throughout the project. Outcomes: stakeholders identified and understood, engagement strategies developed, stakeholders actively involved, satisfaction achieved. Related methodologies: stakeholder-register, stakeholder-engagement.

section.title: 2. Team
section.p: Purpose: establish and maintain a high-performing project team. Outcomes: shared ownership, collaborative environment, continuous development, distributed leadership. Related methodologies: raci-matrix, team-development, communications-management.

section.title: 3. Development Approach
section.p: Purpose: select and maintain the optimal delivery approach. Outcomes: approach aligned with project needs, continuous improvement incorporated. Approach options: Predictive (clear requirements, low uncertainty), Iterative (need to refine solution), Incremental (deliver partial value early), Agile (high uncertainty, need flexibility), Hybrid (mixed characteristics). Related methodologies: agile-hybrid-approaches.

section.title: 4. Planning
section.p: Purpose: plan how project work will be done. Outcomes: organised scope, realistic schedule and budget, resource plans. Related methodologies: work-breakdown-structure, schedule-development, cost-estimation, scope-management.

section.title: 5. Project Work
section.p: Purpose: establish processes and perform work efficiently. Outcomes: efficient processes, appropriate communication, resources managed, procurements handled. Related methodologies: change-control, procurement-management.

section.title: 6. Delivery
section.p: Purpose: deliver value and outcomes that stakeholders accept. Outcomes: scope completed, quality met, benefits realised. Related methodologies: quality-management, benefits-realization, project-closure.

section.title: 7. Measurement
section.p: Purpose: assess project performance and take action. Outcomes: reliable decision information, timely data, performance forecasts. Related methodologies: earned-value-management, lessons-learned.

section.title: 8. Uncertainty
section.p: Purpose: address uncertainty and risk proactively. Outcomes: threats reduced, opportunities captured, reserves appropriate. Related methodologies: risk-register, risk-management.
